Your Future Starts in Türkiye

Aspire Education guides international students through every step of the university application process — from choosing the right program to receiving your acceptance letter.

The Process

Four steps to your acceptance letter

We handle the complexity so you can focus on what matters — your future.

01
Free Consultation
We assess your academic background and match you with the best-fit universities and programs in Türkiye.
02
Application Prep
Our advisors help you prepare every document — transcripts, essays, and language requirements — perfectly.
03
Submission & Tracking
We submit your applications and keep you informed at every stage, handling follow-ups on your behalf.
04
Visa & Arrival
Once accepted, we guide you through the student visa process and help you settle in before your first day.
Our Partner Universities

Our Partner Universities Across Turkey

From health sciences to engineering, law to fine arts — our partner universities cover every field, taught in English and Turkish.

Istinye University
Est. 2015 · Topkapı, Istanbul

Backed by MLP Care Group — Liv Hospital, Medical Park & VM Medical Park — ISU offers students direct clinical training in 2 university hospitals. Ranked #1 in Turkey for International Outlook (THE 2026) and 1001–1200 globally.

MedicineHealth SciencesEngineering165 Programs
Bahçeşehir University (BAU)
Est. 1998 · Beşiktaş, Istanbul

A true global institution on the shores of the Bosphorus. Part of BAU Global with campuses in Berlin, Toronto, Washington DC & more. Ranked 801–1000 globally (THE 2026), with a unique shuttle boat between campuses and 8,000+ international students from 127 countries.

BusinessEngineeringMedicineArchitecture
Istanbul Medipol University
Est. 2009 · Kavacık & Haliç, Istanbul

One of Turkey's fastest-rising research universities — 9th nationally, 801–1000 globally (THE 2026). Home to 40,000+ students from 130 countries, 9 affiliated hospitals including a JCI-accredited mega hospital, and SABITA — among Europe's most advanced research centres.

MedicineDentistryPharmacyEngineering
Istanbul Bilgi University
Est. 1996 · Santral & Dolapdere, Istanbul

One of Turkey's most prestigious private universities, ranked 801–1000 globally (THE 2026). Known for Law, Communications, Engineering and Social Sciences — all primarily taught in English — with a modern campus in a beautifully restored historical powerhouse complex.

LawCommunicationsEngineeringSocial Sciences
Biruni University
Est. 2014 · Topkapı, Istanbul

A medical-focused university with its own fully equipped teaching hospital. Internationally recognised across the Arab world (Iraq, Egypt, Yemen, Libya) and Europe. Offers Medicine, Dentistry, and Health Sciences in English and Turkish with state-of-the-art laboratories.

MedicineDentistryPhysiotherapyPharmacy
Istanbul Aydın University
Est. 2007 · Küçükçekmece, Istanbul

Known especially for Medicine and Health Sciences, with advanced training hospitals and international accreditation recognised in Iraq, Jordan, Egypt, Libya and Sudan. Offers 100+ programs across 13 faculties with a strong focus on English-medium instruction.

MedicineEngineeringArchitectureBusiness
Istanbul Gelişim University
Est. 2008 · Avcılar, Istanbul

Turkey's most internationally accredited private university — 65 programs accredited by AQAS (Germany), ABET (USA), and UK bodies. Ranked 7th in Turkey by Round University Rankings, with 30,000+ students, 100+ laboratories and partnerships with 130+ global universities.

EngineeringDentistryBusinessAviation
Istanbul Beykent University
Est. 1997 · Beylikdüzü, Istanbul

A comprehensive university offering 100+ programs across engineering, business, health sciences, fine arts and aviation. Known for its flexibility — programs available in both English and Turkish — and a welcoming environment for international students.

EngineeringBusinessFine ArtsAviation
Nişantaşı University
Est. 2009 · Şişli, Istanbul

Located in the heart of Istanbul's fashion and cultural district, Nişantaşı offers over 100 programs — including some of Turkey's best design, fashion, and arts programs — at highly competitive fees. A popular choice for creative and business-oriented students alike.

Design & FashionBusinessEngineeringHealth
Istanbul Okan University
Est. 1999 · Tuzla, Istanbul

Renowned for Aviation and Engineering, with ABET accreditation for engineering programs globally recognised by employers. Located on the Asian side of Istanbul near Sabiha Gökçen Airport, with programs in Medicine, Law, and Business. A leader in applied sciences.

AviationEngineeringMedicineLaw
Istanbul Atlas University
Est. 2018 · Kağıthane, Istanbul

One of Istanbul's newest universities, built around the concept of the "butterfly effect" — small actions, transformative outcomes. Features a 110,000m² Valley Campus with 38 advanced labs and Atlas Creative Labs at the intersection of engineering, arts and AI. Ranked 15th among Turkish foundation universities for green campus sustainability.

MedicineEngineeringAI & TechnologyPsychology
Istanbul Arel University
Est. 2007 · Büyükçekmece, Istanbul

Located on Istanbul's peaceful European coastline, Arel offers a broad range of undergraduate, graduate and doctoral programs across Business, Law, Health Sciences and Engineering. Known for its student-friendly environment and affordable fees for international students.

BusinessLawHealth SciencesEngineering
Istanbul Kent University
Est. 2016 · Fatih, Istanbul

One of Istanbul's newer universities, rapidly growing with a focus on student support and career development. Offers programs in Business Administration, Psychology, Law, Engineering and Health Sciences at highly competitive fees — among the most affordable in Istanbul.

BusinessPsychologyLawEngineering
Işık University
Est. 1996 · Şile, Istanbul

Nestled in the green forests of Şile on the Black Sea coast, Işık is celebrated for Engineering excellence — most programs MÜDEK-accredited to international standards. A boutique institution offering a close-knit campus community, strong industry connections and a serene campus setting just an hour from central Istanbul.

EngineeringArchitectureBusinessFine Arts
Beykoz University
Est. 2016 · Beykoz, Istanbul

Turkey's specialist Logistics and Supply Chain university, the only higher education institution in Turkey dedicated to this field. Set in the historic Beykoz district on the Asian shores of the Bosphorus, it offers focused programs in Logistics, Business and Technology — ideal for students targeting global trade careers.

LogisticsBusinessAviationTechnology
Üsküdar University
Est. 2011 · Üsküdar, Istanbul

Turkey's premier university for Neuroscience, Psychology and Health Sciences — with dedicated research centres in Neuroscience, Addiction Rehabilitation, Forensic Science and AI. Located on the serene Asian shore of the Bosphorus, it runs one of Turkey's most active graduate schools in Health and Behavioural Sciences.

NeurosciencePsychologyNursingEngineering
Yeditepe University
Est. 1996 · Kayışdağı, Istanbul (Asian side)

One of Istanbul's largest and most prestigious private universities, founded in 1996 with a purpose-built modern campus on the Asian side. Offers 60+ programs across medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, engineering, law, business and fine arts — with strong English-medium instruction and extensive international partnerships.

MedicineDentistryLawEngineering
Kadir Has University
Est. 1997 · Cibali, Istanbul

Located in a beautifully restored 1923 tobacco factory on the Golden Horn, Kadir Has is known for Communications, Law, Engineering and Business. One of Turkey's oldest private universities, with a vibrant campus in the historic Cibali waterfront district and strong English-medium programs.

CommunicationsLawEngineeringBusiness
Istanbul Kültür University
Est. 1997 · Bakırköy, Istanbul

Founded in 1997 in Bakırköy on Istanbul's European coast, Istanbul Kültür University offers a comprehensive range of programs in Engineering, Business, Law, Architecture, Fine Arts and Health Sciences. MÜDEK-accredited engineering programs, strong international partnerships and competitive fees make it a popular choice.

EngineeringArchitectureBusinessLaw
Haliç University
Est. 1998 · Eyüpsultan, Istanbul

Founded in 1998 by the Bizim Lösemili Çocuklar Vakfı (Our Leukemia Children Foundation), Haliç University occupies a unique place in Turkish higher education — its revenues support childhood leukemia treatment. Offers engineering, health sciences, business, fine arts and dentistry programs on its Golden Horn campus.

EngineeringDentistryHealth SciencesBusiness
Istanbul Altınbaş University
Est. 2008 · Bağcılar, Istanbul

Originally founded as Istanbul Kemerburgaz University in 2008 and renamed Altınbaş University in 2017, this rapidly growing institution offers Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Engineering, Law and Business. Known for affordable fees, modern facilities and a welcoming international student community in Bağcılar.

MedicineDentistryPharmacyEngineering
Istanbul Topkapı University
Est. 2009 · Zeytinburnu, Istanbul

Founded in 2009 (originally as Plato Vocational School), Istanbul Topkapı University has grown to offer 35+ undergraduate and 25+ graduate programs across Fine Arts, Engineering, Economics, Education, Health Sciences and Social Sciences. Recognised in Arab countries and offers competitive tuition fees.

Fine ArtsEngineeringHealth SciencesEconomics
Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University
Est. 2010 · Küçükçekmece, Istanbul

Founded in 2010 by the İlim Yayma Vakfı (Foundation for the Propagation of Science), Istanbul Sabahattin Zaim University is known for its Islamic values-oriented education alongside modern academic programs in Engineering, Business, Food Sciences, Health Sciences and Social Sciences. Internationally recognised and research-active.

EngineeringFood SciencesBusinessHealth Sciences
Istanbul Commerce University
Est. 2001 · Küçükyalı & Üsküdar, Istanbul

Founded in 2001 by Turkey's chambers of commerce, Istanbul Commerce University is dedicated to commercial sciences with programs in Business, Economics, Law, Logistics, Engineering and Communications. It maintains close ties with the Turkish business community, giving students exceptional internship and career placement opportunities.

BusinessEconomicsLawLogistics
Fatih Sultan Mehmet University
Est. 2010 · Küçükçekmece, Istanbul

Named after the Ottoman sultan who conquered Constantinople, Fatih Sultan Mehmet University combines a rich historical identity with modern academic programs in Engineering, Business, Architecture, Fine Arts, Education and Social Sciences. Known for its Islamic heritage studies, strong arts programs and warm campus community.

EngineeringArchitectureFine ArtsEducation
Doğuş University
Est. 1997 · Kadıköy, Istanbul (Asian side)

Founded in 1997 by the Doğuş Group — one of Turkey's leading conglomerates — Doğuş University enjoys strong industry connections that translate into exceptional internship and employment pathways for graduates. Located on the Asian side in Kadıköy, it offers Engineering, Business, Architecture, Law and Communications with English-medium instruction.

EngineeringBusinessArchitectureLaw
Esenyurt University
Est. 2011 · Esenyurt, Istanbul

Istanbul Esenyurt University is one of the most affordable private universities in Istanbul, offering a broad range of programs in Health Sciences, Business, Architecture and Social Sciences at highly competitive tuition fees. Located in Esenyurt on Istanbul's European side, it is especially popular with students seeking accessible quality education.

Health SciencesBusinessArchitectureSocial Sciences
Fenerbahçe University
Est. 2016 · Kadıköy, Istanbul (Asian side)

Founded by one of Turkey's most iconic football clubs, Fenerbahçe University brings a unique sports heritage to higher education. Located in Kadıköy on the Asian side, it offers programs in Sports Sciences, Business, Engineering, Health Sciences and Social Sciences, with exceptional sports facilities and a vibrant student community.

Sports SciencesBusinessEngineeringHealth Sciences
Istanbul Gedik University
Est. 2012 · Kartal, Istanbul (Asian side)

Located in Kartal on Istanbul's Asian side, Istanbul Gedik University was established in 2012 by the Gedik Education and Social Assistance Foundation. It offers a career-oriented range of programs in Engineering, Business, Social Sciences and Fine Arts, with a focus on preparing students for the workforce through strong industry partnerships and practical training.

EngineeringBusinessFine ArtsSocial Sciences
Özyeğin University
Est. 2007 · Çekmeköy, Istanbul

Founded in 2007 by entrepreneur Hüsnü M. Özyeğin, Özyeğin University is recognised as Turkey's most entrepreneurial university, ranked 801–1000 globally (THE). Its modern campus in Çekmeköy offers English-medium programs in Engineering, Business, Architecture and Hospitality, with a strong emphasis on innovation, entrepreneurship and industry collaboration.

EngineeringBusinessArchitectureHospitality
Ankara Medipol University
Est. 2018 · Altındağ, Ankara

Part of the Medipol Group — Turkey's largest private healthcare and education network — Ankara Medipol University was established in 2018 and offers Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Nursing, Engineering and Business. Accredited across Europe and Arab countries including Egypt, Somalia, Sudan and Yemen. Teaching in both English and Turkish.

MedicineDentistryPharmacyEngineering
Ankara Science University
Est. 2020 · Ankara

Founded in 2020, Ankara Science University is built on an innovative, research-first educational philosophy that encourages critical and creative thinking. All programs are taught in English across Engineering & Architecture, Fine Arts & Design, Humanities & Social Sciences, and Law — making it a premier all-English destination in Turkey's capital.

EngineeringDesignLawSocial Sciences
Atılım University
Est. 1997 · İncek, Ankara

Established in 1997 in Ankara's green İncek district, Atılım University is one of Turkey's leading engineering and business universities with a fully English-medium campus. It offers over 50 programs including Medicine, Engineering, Aviation (Pilot Training), Business and Law — with MÜDEK-accredited engineering and strong global partnerships.

MedicineEngineeringAviationBusiness
Bilkent University
Est. 1984 · Çankaya, Ankara

Turkey's first private university, founded in 1984, Bilkent is consistently ranked among the top 500 universities globally (QS) and is one of Turkey's most prestigious institutions. Its fully English-medium campus near Ankara offers world-class programs in Engineering, Business, Law, Medicine, Fine Arts and Science — with exceptional research output and international faculty.

EngineeringBusinessLawMedicine
Hasan Kalyoncu University
Est. 2008 · Şahinbey, Gaziantep

Located in Gaziantep — Turkey's industrial heartland and gateway to the Middle East — Hasan Kalyoncu University was founded in 2008 and offers Architecture, Engineering, Business, Health Sciences, Law and Education. Particularly well-positioned for students from Syria, Iraq and the wider Arab world, with strong regional recognition and Arabic-friendly support services.

ArchitectureEngineeringHealth SciencesBusiness
Koç University
Est. 1993 · Sarıyer, Istanbul

Turkey's top-ranked private research university (QS top 500). Founded in 1993 by the Koç Group — Turkey's largest conglomerate — with a fully English-medium campus in Sarıyer on the Bosphorus. World-class programs in Business, Engineering, Medicine, Law and Sciences with exceptional research output.

EngineeringBusinessMedicineLaw
Maltepe University
Est. 1997 · Maltepe, Istanbul (Asian side)

Founded in 1997 on Istanbul's Asian coastline, Maltepe University offers a scenic Bosphorus campus with programs in Medicine, Dentistry, Law, Engineering, Fine Arts and Psychology. Known for its arts and communications tradition, strong international student support and highly competitive tuition fees.

MedicineDentistryLawFine Arts
Sabancı University
Est. 1994 · Tuzla, Istanbul

One of Turkey's most prestigious private universities (QS top 500), founded in 1994 by the Sabancı Group. Known for its interdisciplinary model — students choose their major after the first year — with excellent Engineering, Management, Arts & Social Sciences programs in English on a stunning campus near the Bosphorus.

EngineeringManagementSocial SciencesArts
Yeni Yüzyıl University
Est. 1996 · Gaziosmanpaşa, Istanbul

Founded in 1996 in Istanbul, Yeni Yüzyıl (New Century) University offers programs in Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Nursing, Engineering and Business across several campuses. Recognised in Arab countries and particularly popular with international students seeking affordable health sciences education in Istanbul.

MedicineDentistryPharmacyNursing
Lokman Hekim University
Est. 2005 · Ankara

Named after Lokman Hekim — the legendary physician of Ottoman folklore — this Ankara university specialises in health sciences. Offers Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Nursing and allied health programs with its own teaching hospital, alongside Engineering and Social Sciences. Recognised in Arab and African countries.

MedicineDentistryPharmacyNursing
Ostim Technical University
Est. 2017 · Ostim, Ankara

Founded in 2017 inside Ostim Organized Industrial Zone — one of Turkey's largest manufacturing hubs — Ostim Technical University is uniquely embedded within industry. Engineering and Technology students have direct access to 5,000+ companies on their doorstep, providing unmatched internship and employment pathways.

EngineeringTechnologyIndustryApplied Sciences
TED University
Est. 2009 · Çankaya, Ankara

Founded in 2009 by the Turkish Education Association (TED) — an institution with 90+ years in Turkish education — TED University delivers high-quality English-medium programs in Engineering, Business, Architecture, Law and Social Sciences. Known for small class sizes, close student-faculty relationships and strong research culture.

EngineeringBusinessArchitectureLaw
Turkish Aeronautical Association University
Est. 1992 · Etimesgut, Ankara

Turkey's premier aviation university, founded by the Turkish Aeronautical Association in 1992. Offers Pilot Training, Aeronautical Engineering, Aviation Management, Air Traffic Control and related programs with its own airfield and flight simulators. A top destination for students pursuing careers in civil aviation.

Pilot TrainingAeronauticsAviationEngineering
Konya Food and Agriculture University
Est. 2015 · Konya

A unique specialist university founded in 2015 in Konya — Turkey's agricultural heartland. Focuses on Food Sciences, Agriculture, Biotechnology and Engineering, fully in English. Backed by major Turkish food industry companies, it offers exceptional career pathways in food production, agricultural technology and sustainability.

Food SciencesAgricultureBiotechnologyEngineering
Toros University
Est. 2009 · Mersin

Located in Mersin on Turkey's Mediterranean coast, Toros University was founded in 2009 and offers programs in Engineering, Business, Health Sciences, Law and Fine Arts. Mersin's position as a major port city and industrial hub gives students strong ties to logistics, trade and maritime industries. Competitive fees and warm Mediterranean climate.

EngineeringBusinessHealth SciencesLaw
Yaşar University
Est. 2001 · Bornova, İzmir

Founded in 2001 in İzmir — Turkey's vibrant Aegean city — Yaşar University offers English-medium programs in Business, Economics, Engineering, Architecture, Communications and Fine Arts. İzmir's cosmopolitan, international character and Mediterranean lifestyle make it one of Turkey's most attractive student cities outside Istanbul.

BusinessEngineeringArchitectureFine Arts
🌊 North Cyprus
Near East University
Est. 1988 · Nicosia, North Cyprus

The largest and most comprehensive university in North Cyprus, founded in 1988 with 20+ faculties and 50,000+ students from 100+ countries. Offers Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Engineering, Business, Law and more — many in English. A full-service campus with its own hospitals, research centres and vibrant student life.

MedicineDentistryEngineeringBusiness
Eastern Mediterranean University
Est. 1979 · Famagusta, North Cyprus

One of the oldest and most prestigious universities in North Cyprus, founded in 1979. EMU offers 200+ programs across all disciplines with a strong emphasis on English-medium instruction. With 18,000+ students from 100+ countries, it is a truly international campus set in the historic city of Famagusta on the Mediterranean coast.

EngineeringBusinessArchitectureEducation
Kyrenia University
Est. 2013 · Kyrenia, North Cyprus

Located in the picturesque coastal city of Kyrenia, this university was founded in 2013 and focuses on Health Sciences, Engineering, Business and Social Sciences. Set against the stunning backdrop of the Kyrenia mountains and Mediterranean Sea, it offers an exceptional quality of life alongside quality academic programs at affordable fees.

Health SciencesEngineeringBusinessSocial Sciences
Bahceşehir Cyprus University
Est. 2014 · Nicosia, North Cyprus

Part of the prestigious BAU Global network, Bahceşehir Cyprus University brings Istanbul Bahceşehir University's world-class standards to North Cyprus. Students benefit from the same global exchange programs, dual degree options and international recognition as the Istanbul campus, in a Mediterranean island setting.

BusinessEngineeringArchitectureHealth Sciences
Cyprus Health and Social Sciences University
Est. 2016 · Güzelyurt, North Cyprus

A specialist health and social sciences university in North Cyprus founded in 2016. Offers focused programs in Medicine, Nursing, Physiotherapy, Psychology, Social Work and allied health fields. Strong practical training orientation with clinical partnerships, and competitive fees for health sciences students seeking Mediterranean island life.

MedicineNursingPsychologyPhysiotherapy
Cyprus Science University
Est. 2016 · Girne, North Cyprus

Founded in 2016 near Kyrenia (Girne), Cyprus Science University offers programs in Engineering, Computer Science, Business, Education and Fine Arts. A growing institution on the Mediterranean coast with modern facilities, an international student community and a strong focus on applied sciences and technology.

EngineeringComputer ScienceBusinessEducation
Cyprus West University
Est. 2015 · Gazimağusa, North Cyprus

Located in Gazimağusa (Famagusta) on the eastern Mediterranean coast, Cyprus West University was founded in 2015. Offers programs in Business, Engineering, Health Sciences, Law and Architecture at highly competitive fees. The university's coastal city setting and multicultural campus make it an appealing destination for international students.

BusinessEngineeringHealth SciencesLaw
